repair_candidate.summary: Repair/proof plan for #90875: verify the zsh-specific path/PATH linkage bug, keep the one-file loop-variable rename if it proves the behavior, and add a focused regression or proof script so the Real behavior proof gate can pass before any merge consideration.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(clawdock-helpers): prove zsh PATH is preserved while ensuring dirs
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- Preserve @lshgdut's one-file fix from #90875 by avoiding zsh's special `path` array name inside `_clawdock_ensure_dir`.
- Add focused behavior proof that sourcing clawdock helpers and ensuring a directory does not mutate `PATH` under zsh.

## Source and credit
- Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90875
- Credit: Thanks @lshgdut for the original diagnosis and patch for zsh `path`/`PATH` linkage.

## Validation
- `zsh -fc 'typeset -a before after; source scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh; before=(${path[@]}); _clawdock_ensure_dir /tmp/openclaw-clawdock-proof; after=(${path[@]}); [[ ${(@j/:/)before} == ${(@j/:/)after} ]]'`
- `npm run validate`
validation_commands: zsh -fc 'typeset -a before after; source scripts/clawdock/clawdock-helpers.sh; before=(${path[@]}); _clawdock_ensure_dir /tmp/openclaw-clawdock-proof; after=(${path[@]}); [[ ${(@j/:/)before} == ${(@j/:/)after} ]]'
credit_notes: Credit @lshgdut for identifying the zsh path/PATH linkage caused by the loop variable name and for the original one-file fix.
